title: Proposal for an offline game in Firefox - Undulo
content:
Dear Mozilla Firefox Team,

My name is Mohamed Yassine Al Mathlouthi, and I am reaching out to share a small independent game project that I believe could be an interesting candidate for a browser offline experience.

The game is called Undulo, a sci-fi action platformer where players control a fearless fox warrior battling alien enemies across floating planets, cosmic debris, and the ruins of a mysterious starship. The project was created in one month for the GitHub Game Off 2025 game jam using the Godot Engine.

Undulo is already available online and open-source:

Play the game: https://crisp-lad.itch.io/undulo

GitHub repository: https://github.com/Foxy-Trot-Games

Given that several browsers include small built-in games for offline moments, I wondered if a lightweight browser version of Undulo could potentially be considered for a similar purpose within Firefox. Since the game is built with Godot, it can be exported to HTML5 and run directly in the browser.

Our small development team would be happy to adapt or optimize the game if such a collaboration were of interest.

Development Team – Foxy Trot Games • Crisp_Lad – Team Lead & Programmer • Koallary – Level Design & Artist • Screen – Programmer • ClownManGuy – Animator & Artist • Foks – Music Designer

Thank you for your time and for the incredible work you do on Firefox and open web technologies. I would be grateful for any feedback or guidance regarding this idea.

Best regards, Mohamed Yassine Al Mathlouthi

title: Changing the sidebar shortcut from the UI
content:
There seems to be no possible way to change the shortcut for peeking at the tabs in the sidebar (when sidebar revamp is enabled) from the about:keyboard UI. I have so far managed to modify the shortcut through intercepting function calls.

What I have done: 1. Read through the JS source code for about:keyboard which says the shortcut ID is `toggleSidebarKb` 2. Added hooks to intercept `RPMSendQuery` calls 3. Modified the shortcut through devtools open on the about:keyboard page with the following snippet

```js await RPMSendQuery("CustomKeys:ChangeKey", { 

 "modifiers": "accel",  "isValid": true,  "key": "S",  "shortcut": "Ctrl+S",  "id": "toggleSidebarKb"

}) ```

The way I am doing it is definitely a hack. I am not sure if I am missing something. Although the shortcuts for bookmarks, history, and GenAI chat sidebar are present, there seems to be no entry to change the sidebar toggle shortcut itself. I'd love to work on this but I haven't been able to mach bootstrap on NixOS.

Thank you for any assistance :)
